title: Compute Total from Equal Addends
description: Find the total by summing equal addends in a repeated addition expression.
condition: Given a repeated addition expression with equal addends.
response: Compute the sum to determine the total.
label: Principle
notes: Requires accurate addition of equal groups.

title: Count Total in Array by Equal Groups
description: Find the total number of objects in a rectangular array by counting equal rows or equal columns.
condition: Given a rectangular array with equal number of objects in each row or each column.
response: Add the number of objects in each equal row (or column) to find the total.
label: Principle
example: An array has 4 rows of 5 circles. Compute $5 + 5 + 5 + 5 = 20$.
notes: Students may count by rows or by columns.

title: Count Total Objects in Array
description: Determine the total number of objects arranged in equal rows and columns.
condition: Given a rectangular array of equal-size squares or objects.
response: Count or compute the total number of objects.
label: Production / Skill
example: An array with 2 rows and 4 columns has $2 \times 4 = 8$ squares.
notes: May be done by counting all or by repeated addition.

title: Find Number of Pairs in a Quantity
description: Determine how many groups of 2 can be made from a given whole number up to 20.
condition: Given a whole number up to 20 and forming pairs.
response: Calculate or count the number of groups of 2 that can be made.
label: Production / Skill
example: From 15 objects, \(15 \div 2 = 7\) pairs with 1 left over.
notes: May be done via repeated subtraction or skip counting by 2.

title: Identify Equal Groups
description: Recognize when objects can be partitioned into two or more groups with the same number in each group.
condition: Given a collection of objects or shapes.
response: State or show equal groups with the same number in each group.
label: Rule / Plan
example: 12 blocks can be split into 2 groups of 6 each.
notes: Used to justify evenness or fair sharing.

title: Identify Equal Rows and Columns
description: Recognize that a rectangular array has the same number of objects in each row and the same number in each column.
condition: Given a rectangular arrangement of objects aligned in rows and columns.
response: State or recognize that each row has the same number and each column has the same number.
label: Concept
example: In a 4 by 5 array, each row has 5 objects and each column has 4 objects.

title: Odd as One Left Over
description: Determine that a number is odd if pairing or grouping leaves exactly one unpaired object.
condition: Given a set of objects that can be paired except for one leftover.
response: Conclude the total number is odd.
label: Principle
example: If 9 circles are paired and 1 circle remains, then 9 is odd.

title: Partition Rectangle into Equal Squares
description: Draw lines to divide a rectangle completely into equal-size squares.
condition: Given a rectangle partially partitioned into equal squares.
response: Draw additional horizontal and vertical lines to form only equal-size squares.
label: Production / Skill
example: Extend grid lines to form a $2 \times 4$ array of 8 equal squares.
notes: Spatial reasoning skill. [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies]

title: Recognize Even by Pairing
description: Determine that a number is even if all objects can be paired with none left over.
condition: Given a set of objects that can be grouped into pairs with no leftovers.
response: Conclude the total number is even.
label: Principle
example: If 8 circles form 4 pairs with none left over, then 8 is even.

title: Represent Array as Repeated Addition
description: Write an equation with equal addends to represent the total number of objects in an array.
condition: Given a rectangular array with known number of rows and columns.
response: Write a repeated addition equation using either rows or columns as equal addends.
label: Principle
notes: Either orientation (rows or columns) is valid.

title: Write Even Number as Two Equal Addends
description: Express an even number as the sum of two identical addends.
response: Write the number in the form $n = a + a$.
label: Principle
